meta.title: Shinsuke Maki | Full-Stack Engineer
meta.description: Full-Stack Engineer with 9 years of experience building reliable, scalable web applications.

home.hero_tagline: I have over 9 years of web development experience and have spent the last 3 years developing a deep interest in Artificial Intelligence (AI) to master advanced AI technologies. I finally left my company to become independent and began working as a freelancer with the courage to believe that I could single-handedly replace a team.

about.intro: Nine years of writing code. Three years of betting on AI. One decision to go independent and carry an entire team on my own. Technology is always the means — building something that truly matters to people is always the point.
about.story:
From the moment I wrote my first line of code, there was a pure, almost childlike joy in making something move on a screen. I still remember the day my first commit went live in production — I pumped my fist quietly at my desk, alone, and thought: this is what I want to do for the rest of my life. That was the beginning of a journey that has now spanned over nine years.

In the startup world, I learned to write code that works under pressure — when requirements change between morning and evening, when deadlines are yesterday, and when there is no safety net. In larger organizations, I felt the weight of responsibility that comes with systems used by hundreds of thousands of people, where a single architectural decision can cascade into an outage. Living both extremes forged the engineer I am today.

Three years ago, everything shifted. I felt AI moving through the industry like a current — quiet at first, then impossible to ignore. I knew that standing on the sidelines was not an option. So I began studying in earnest: reading research papers late into the night, integrating LLM APIs into side projects, experimenting with prompt engineering, fine-tuning, and retrieval-augmented generation. Gradually, AI stopped being a curiosity and became a core part of my toolkit.

Then came the hardest decision of my career. A stable salary, familiar colleagues, the comfort of belonging to something established — I walked away from all of it. Not recklessly, but with a clear-eyed conviction: that the combination of deep web engineering experience and real-world AI capability could make a single person as effective as an entire team. I left to prove that.

Today, as a freelance engineer, I am living that proof — one project at a time. Technology is always the means; the end is always the same: to build something that genuinely helps people. That purpose has never changed since the day I wrote that first line of code.
about.value_quality_desc: Clean, readable, and maintainable code that stands the test of time.
about.value_reliability_desc: Systems that work correctly under real-world conditions.
about.value_simplicity_desc: The simplest solution that solves the problem effectively.
about.value_learning_desc: Staying current with the evolving landscape of web technology.
